channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pete Buttigieg won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, leaving door wide open for 2028 run
item.description: SAUGATUCK, Mich. (AP) — Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced that he will not seek an open U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, a move that leaves the door wide open for him to seek a much bigger role as his party’s next presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing American in Dominican Republic: No suspects as search for Sudiksha Konanki reaches one week
item.description: Dominican authorities have expanded the search for missing Sudiksha Konanki, a UPitt student from Virginia who vanished from the Riu Republica resort a week ago on spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic-led states sue to block Trump administration layoffs at the Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of Democratic-led states is challenging the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs across the Education Department, saying it amounts to an illegal dismantling of an agency created by Congress.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nikki Glaser to return as host of Golden Globes in 2026
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After a well-reviewed emcee debut, the Golden Globes are bringing back comedian Nikki Glaser to host the 2026 ceremony. Dick Clark Productions, the producer of the award show, announced Thursday that Glaser will return for the 83rd Globes next January.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man ate patients at South Carolina mental health facility: Lawsuit
item.description: The family blames the facility for allowing what they describe as "a budding serial killer" to be placed in a home with vulnerable adults.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, NewsNation

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim president on Thursday signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AT&T wants to sell you a "landline" again
item.description: AT&T is launching a new service called AT&T Phone Advanced, a modern take on the traditional phone line. The service is now available in Southern California. It's a special box that uses a cellular or broadband connection instead of a traditional wired phone line.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department launches criminal investigation into 2 NYC migrant shelters
item.description: The Justice Department is investigating funding and operations at two Manhattan hotels being used as migrant shelters, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lessons learned in Natalee Holloway disappearance apply to Sudiksha Konanki case: retired FBI agent
item.description: A former FBI special agent says investigators can use lessons learned from previous missing person's cases like Natalee Holloway's to find Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-Texas megachurch pastor indicted in Oklahoma on child sex abuse charges
item.description: A former pastor of a Texas megachurch who resigned after a woman accused him of sexually abusing her in the 1980s has been indicted in Oklahoma on child sexual abuse charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Delphi murders: Full 'Bridge Guy' video releases on Richard Allen innocence website
item.description: A full, 43-second video taken by Libby German just before she and her friend Abby Williams were murdered has released online. The video can be seen above.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: The fate of the IRS Direct File program remains unclear as Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency cleave their way through the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blue state city council reconsidering trans member’s nomination after discovering OnlyFans account: report
item.description: City councilors in Lynnwood, Washington, are considering reversing the appointment of a new member after learning of their OnlyFans page.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From soup cans to airplanes, steel and aluminum are a fundamental part of American life
item.description: Steel and aluminum are ubiquitous in Americans' lives. A stainless steel refrigerator holds aluminum soda cans. A stainless steel drum tumbles inside an aluminum washing machine. They're the metals used in cars and airplanes, phones and frying pans, skyscrapers and zippers.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE runs out of space, money to detain migrants, report says
item.description: A high-ranking official with Immigration and Customs Enforcement says the agency has run out of money to detain and house more migrants for the time being, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where is Kelly Clarkson? Fans concerned
item.description: Kelly Clarkson's rep didn't return NewsNation's emails, but sources suggest she could be overwhelmed.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Anonymous donor paying to spay, neuter 120 pit bulls in Denver
item.description: "What we're trying to do is just make sure that everybody's got equal access to the resources they need to do right by their pet.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Fierro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 20 charged with murder after 3 die inside Nevada prison
item.description: Charges against the 20 defendants include first-degree murder and attempted murder with gang enhancements, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: California Gov Newsom sets Menendez brothers parole board hearing date in bid for clemency
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on his podcast Tuesday that Erik and Lyle Menendez will have a parole board hearing on June 13 as they push for clemency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutors say Kohberger's defense will argue evidence was planted in Idaho student murders case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors say Bryan Kohberger’s defense team will argue at his murder trial that someone else could have planted a knife sheath with Kohberger’s DNA at the home where four University of Idaho students were killed in 2022.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeanne Shaheen to retire, setting up battle for New Hampshire Senate seat
item.description: “It was a difficult decision, made more difficult by the current environment in the country — by President Trump and what he’s doing right now,” said Shaheen, who is 78.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: How to photograph the total lunar eclipse
item.description: You could see the full moon turn blood red (or burnt red) early Friday morning as the earth transits between the moon and the sun. And if luck is on your side, there will not be any clouds covering this celestial sight.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Phenix, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Haven police reconnect father with daughter who was kidnapped in 1999
item.description: New Haven police have reconnected a father and his daughter who was kidnapped decades ago from New Haven.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KRON4

item.title: FBI warns of extortion and self-harm networks targeting minors
item.description: FBI Denver said that 764, a decentralized and transnational sextortion network, is seeing an increase in activity, as well as other violent online networks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4
